members.name: AddDirectory
members.signature: public int AddDirectory(String path, int permissions, int modifiedTime)
members.summary: Adds directory to the archive. If [param modified_time] is set to [code]0[/code], current system time is used. [b]Note:[/b] Directories are automatically created when [method start_file] is called, use this function before adding files to create directories with custom permissions and modification time.

members.name: Close
members.summary: Closes the underlying resources used by this instance.

members.name: CloseFile
members.summary: Stops writing to a file within the archive. It will fail if there is no open file.

members.name: Open
members.signature: public int Open(String path, int append)
members.summary: Opens a zip file for writing at the given path using the specified write mode. This must be called before everything else.

members.name: StartFile
members.signature: public int StartFile(String path, int permissions, int modifiedTime)
members.summary: Starts writing to a file within the archive. Only one file can be written at the same time. If [param modified_time] is set to [code]0[/code], current system time is used. Must be called after [method open].

members.name: WriteFile
members.summary: Write the given [param data] to the file. Needs to be called after [method start_file].

members.name: CompressionLevel
members.summary: The compression level used when [method start_file] is called. Use [enum ZIPPacker.CompressionLevel] as a reference.

values.name: COMPRESSION_BEST
values.summary: Start a file with the best Deflate compression level ([code]9[/code]). This is slow to compress, but results in smaller file sizes than [constant COMPRESSION_DEFAULT]. Decompression speed is generally unaffected by the chosen compression level.

values.name: COMPRESSION_DEFAULT
values.summary: Start a file with the default Deflate compression level ([code]6[/code]). This is a good compromise between speed and file size.

values.name: COMPRESSION_FAST
values.summary: Start a file with the fastest Deflate compression level ([code]1[/code]). This is fast to compress, but results in larger file sizes than [constant COMPRESSION_DEFAULT]. Decompression speed is generally unaffected by the chosen compression level.

values.name: COMPRESSION_NONE
values.summary: Start a file with no compression. This is also known as the "Store" compression mode and is the fastest method of packing files inside a ZIP archive. Consider using this mode for files that are already compressed (such as JPEG, PNG, MP3, or Ogg Vorbis files).

values.name: APPEND_ADDINZIP
values.summary: Add new files to the existing zip archive at the given path.

values.name: APPEND_CREATE
values.summary: Create a new zip archive at the given path.

values.name: APPEND_CREATEAFTER
values.summary: Append a new zip archive to the end of the already existing file at the given path.

constants.name: APPEND_CREATE
constants.summary: Create a new zip archive at the given path.

constants.name: APPEND_CREATEAFTER
constants.summary: Append a new zip archive to the end of the already existing file at the given path.

constants.name: APPEND_ADDINZIP
constants.summary: Add new files to the existing zip archive at the given path.

constants.name: COMPRESSION_DEFAULT
constants.summary: Start a file with the default Deflate compression level ([code]6[/code]). This is a good compromise between speed and file size.

constants.name: COMPRESSION_NONE
constants.summary: Start a file with no compression. This is also known as the "Store" compression mode and is the fastest method of packing files inside a ZIP archive. Consider using this mode for files that are already compressed (such as JPEG, PNG, MP3, or Ogg Vorbis files).

constants.name: COMPRESSION_FAST
constants.summary: Start a file with the fastest Deflate compression level ([code]1[/code]). This is fast to compress, but results in larger file sizes than [constant COMPRESSION_DEFAULT]. Decompression speed is generally unaffected by the chosen compression level.

constants.name: COMPRESSION_BEST
constants.summary: Start a file with the best Deflate compression level ([code]9[/code]). This is slow to compress, but results in smaller file sizes than [constant COMPRESSION_DEFAULT]. Decompression speed is generally unaffected by the chosen compression level.
